title: thymus serpyllum subsp. effusus
intro: Thymus serpyllum subsp. effusus, also known as wild thyme, is a perennial herb belonging to the Lamiaceae family. It is commonly used in herbal medicine due to its antimicrobial, antiseptic, and antifungal properties, which can help combat infections and promote wound healing. The health benefits of wild thyme include its ability to alleviate respiratory issues such as coughs and colds, as well as its anti-inflammatory properties, which can help reduce pain and swelling. Wild thyme can be prepared in various forms, including teas, infusions, and tinctures, which can be used to support immune function and overall well-being.
